module.title: Investment Fundamentals
module.description: Learn how investing works, how to manage risk, and how to build a diversified portfolio for long-term wealth creation.
learning_objectives: Understand major investment types and how they work
Learn how risk and return are connected
Build a diversified portfolio based on goals and timeline
Apply long-term investing strategies like dollar-cost averaging

lessons.title: Understanding Investment Options
lessons.content: Investing involves putting money into assets like stocks, bonds, ETFs, and mutual funds. Stocks offer higher potential returns but higher risk, while bonds are more stable but lower return. ETFs and mutual funds allow diversification in a single investment.

lessons.title: Risk vs Return Relationship
lessons.content: Higher returns usually come with higher risk. Your risk tolerance depends on your age, income stability, and financial goals. Younger investors can typically take more risk because they have more time to recover from losses.

lessons.title: Inflation and Buying Power
lessons.content: Inflation reduces the value of money over time. This means cash loses purchasing power if it is not invested. Investing helps your money grow faster than inflation.

lessons.title: Building a Diversified Portfolio
lessons.content: A good portfolio spreads investments across different asset classes to reduce risk. Diversification helps protect your money from market volatility.

lessons.title: Dollar-Cost Averaging & Rebalancing
lessons.content: Dollar-cost averaging means investing a fixed amount regularly, reducing the impact of market volatility. Rebalancing keeps your portfolio aligned with your goals.

lessons.title: Common Investing Mistakes
lessons.content: Many investors lose money by panic selling, chasing trends, or trying to time the market. Successful investing requires patience and consistency.

questions.question: Why is diversification important in investing?
questions.explanation: Diversification reduces risk by spreading investments across different assets.

questions.question: What is the main benefit of dollar-cost averaging?
options: Buying at the lowest price always
questions.explanation: Dollar-cost averaging smooths out market volatility by investing consistently over time.

questions.question: What happens to purchasing power when inflation increases?
questions.explanation: Inflation reduces purchasing power over time.
